content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 13, 1993

Craig T. Olson
800 San Juan Lane
Placentia, Ca 92670

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-93-230

Dear Mr. Olson:

You have requested advice concerning the campaign provisions
of the Political Reform Act ("the Act").1/ Since your question is
general in nature, we are treating your request as one for
informal assistance.2/

QUESTION

What is the fair market value for the use of a bulk mailing
permit?

CONCLUSION

The fair market value is the price paid for the bulk mailing
permit or the difference in postage costs between the bulk mail
postage rate and the regular mail rate.

FACTS

You are currently a member of the Placentia-Yorba Linda
Unified School District. In the November 1992 election your
committee used the bulk mailing permit that is owned by your
business. The annual cost of the permit is approximately $150.
Following the Commission's advice, the difference between the bulk
mail postage rate and the first class rate ($5,725) was reported

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section
18329(c)(3).)

I-92-230
Page 2


as an in-kind contribution received by your committee from your
business. For purposes of future elections, you are asking the
Commission to reconsider this advice and declare the annual value
of the bulk rate permit as the fair market value of the in-kind
contribution.

ANALYSIS

A candidate's use of another person's bulk mail permit is an
in-kind contribution and its fair market value must be disclosed.
(Sections 82015 and 82025.5.) The Commission has advised that the
fair market value is the difference between the bulk mail postage
rate and the regular postage rate the candidate would be required
to pay. (Wexer Advice Letter, No. I-91-531, Alsop Advice Letter,
No. I-91-499, Yu Advice Letter, No. I-86-25.) However, because a
bulk mail permit is readily obtainable by the general public,
including candidates and committees, the price of the same or
similar bulk mail permit may also be used for purposes of
determining the fair market value of the contribution.

If you have questions concerning this letter, please contact
me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Lynda Cassady
Assistant Division Chief
Technical Assistance Division

postage rate and the regular mail rate.
V

QUESTION: What is the fair market value for the use of
a bulk mailing
permit?

CONCLUSION: The fair market value is the price paid for the bulk mailing
permit or the difference in postage costs between the bulk mail
postage rate and the regular mail rate.
V
